summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 10 warning, 9 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:111` — `args` forwarded verbatim to `runSingleFeature`; `run.mjs:790` reads `--dry-run` from those args, so `agt cron-tick --dry-run` silently runs features in dry-run mode with no documentation or guard; pass `[]` or a filtered allowlist instead

🟡 `.team/features/cron-based-outer-loop/` — No SPEC.md exists; acceptance criteria are inferred from the task title only, with no PM-approved requirements document — create a minimal SPEC.md for traceability

🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:89` — SIGKILL mid-run leaves the board item permanently "in-progress"; only graceful failures trigger revert — add pre-flight stale "in-progress" recovery (carry-forward from architect review)

🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:100` — Title sanitization misses Unicode line separators U+2028 and U+2029; the code's own comment labels this as an anti-injection security control — extend regex to include `\u0085\u2028\u2029` (carry-forward from security review)

Task-4 additionally hardened the comment path: `_commentIssue` is now wrapped in try-catch and its return value is checked — closing two 🟡 carry-forward warnings that had been flagged across multiple prior review rounds.

No critical (🔴) findings. The three 🟡 backlog items are real but don't regress currently shipped behavior. The operationally significant one is the stranded in-progress board item on SIGKILL — users relying on the board as a scheduler will need to manually revert if cron-tick is killed mid-run.

🟡 `test/cron-tick.test.mjs:90,185,243,271` — `console.log/warn/error` restored inline after `await cmdCronTick()`, not in a `try/finally`; an unexpected throw leaves the mock live for subsequent tests — move restores to `afterEach` or wrap in `try/finally`

🟡 `test/cron-tick.test.mjs:86,103,134,176,199,237,262,286,314` — `lock.release` stub is a no-op with no spy; the `finally` at `cron.mjs:135` is never asserted to execute in any test — add a spy and assert `release()` was called in both the success and failure paths

**Why PASS and not ITERATE:** The two 🟡 items are test hygiene gaps (console leak risk, missing spy assertion), not missing coverage of the core acceptance criteria. The failure path, revert behavior, commentIssue try-catch, and title sanitization are all directly tested and passing.

🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:100` — Title sanitization misses Unicode line separators U+2028, U+2029, and U+0085, which LLMs treat as structural newlines and are valid prompt-injection vectors; the code's own comment marks this as an intentional security control — extend the regex to include `\u0085\u2028\u2029`

🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:20` — `readProjectNumber` duplicates `outer-loop.mjs:117` body-for-body (same regex, same try/catch, same return contract); extract to a shared utility to avoid split maintenance for the project-URL regex
